hyperedges.label: Cloudflare Workers Durable Object Architecture
hyperedges.description: Worker fetch handler acts as HTTP router delegating sync and conference API calls to Durable Objects (ChurchSyncDO, ConferenceDO) via env bindings, with both DOs using SQLite storage and sharing the Env interface pattern.

hyperedges.label: Dev Proxy and Test Pipeline
hyperedges.description: Vite dev server proxies /api to wrangler dev on port 8787 which serves worker_entry; Vitest references wrangler.jsonc for worker pool config, and smoke_test exercises the same worker via SELF.fetch.

nodes.label: Theobase Platform
nodes.rationale: A platform for Seventh-day Adventist church administration covering operations from Conference down to Member, with read-only aggregated reporting up to General Conference level. Modular by design with Finance, Membership, Organization Structure, Auth/Roles, and Audit/Reports as initial modules.

nodes.label: ADR-0001: Append-Only Immutable Finance
nodes.rationale: The SDA Church Manual requires dual-custody on all funds and annual audit of church records. An editable financial ledger would undermine the audit trail because a bad actor could silently modify past entries. Append-only guarantees that every action leaves a permanent, verifiable record. Rejected mutable transactions with soft-delete because: (a) the audit log could be tampered with in the same mutable system, (b) the dual-custody requirement implies finality once two people confirm a batch, and (c) append-only is simpler with fewer code paths to secure.

nodes.label: ADR-0002: Per-Conference D1 Tenancy
nodes.rationale: A Conference is the natural administrative boundary â€” Conference officers see all their churches' data; members and churches in different Conferences never interact. Per-Conference isolation simplifies access control (row-level security per church_id within the database rather than cross-tenant filters), limits blast radius, and maps to D1's horizontal scaling model (thousands of small databases are free; one massive database hits the 10GB ceiling). A typical 500-church Conference with 10 years of transactions fits in under 1GB. Rejected per-church D1 (NÃ— complexity for cross-church operations) and single global D1 (D1 single-threaded throughput limits and 10GB ceiling).

nodes.label: ADR-0003: Offline-First PWA Architecture
nodes.rationale: Many SDA churches operate in rural areas with intermittent or no internet during Sabbath services â€” exactly when financial data is collected. An online-only app would be unusable at the moment of highest need. The PWA model allows installation on any device without an app store, critical for global reach across 215+ countries. Trade-offs accepted: added complexity from conflict resolution and dual-custody enforcement across disconnected clients, eventual consistency with stale data until sync completes, and no native mobile app in MVP. Rejected online-only SaaS with optimistic UI because it is unusable in the primary deployment environment.

nodes.label: ADR-0004: Modular Package Architecture
nodes.rationale: The initial scope is Finance + Membership + Organization + Auth + Audit, but the SDA church has many more departments (Sabbath School, Pathfinders, Health Ministries, Youth, Evangelism, ADRA, Family Ministries, Music, Communication, etc.). A monolith would accumulate all of these in one ever-growing codebase. Modules allow each department to be developed and deployed independently, sharing only the core layer (auth, org hierarchy, member records, audit trail). Modules are composed at build time, not hot-plugged at runtime, which is simpler and sufficient for single-tenant deploy. The shared core is a hard dependency â€” a breaking change to core affects all modules, but the core is small and stable.

nodes.label: Theobase Brand Cover Image (1640x720 OpenGraph/Social Share)
nodes.rationale: 1640x720 PNG image in branding directory for Theobase, the Seventh-day Adventist church administration platform. Dimensions match standard Open Graph / social share cover image (2:1 aspect ratio). Companion files in the same directory: logo-light.svg (orange #F97316 + gray #6B7280 wordmark), icon.svg (flame/torch icon in orange), favicon.ico, profile-picture.png, and theobase.af (Affinity Designer source). Actual visual content of cover.png is AMBIGUOUS â€” model cannot view images.
nodes.norm_label: theobase brand cover image (1640x720 opengraph/social share)

nodes.label: Theobase Brand Color Palette: Orange #F97316 + Gray #6B7280
nodes.rationale: Brand colors extracted from companion SVG files in the same branding directory. Logo-light.svg uses fill:rgb(249,115,22) for the icon and fill:rgb(107,114,128) for the wordmark text. The icon.svg uses fill:rgb(249,115,22) exclusively.
nodes.norm_label: theobase brand color palette: orange #f97316 + gray #6b7280

nodes.label: Theobase SDA Church Administration Platform
nodes.rationale: Theobase is a platform for Seventh-day Adventist church administration from Conference down to Member level, with modular architecture (Finance, Membership, Organization Structure, Auth/Roles, Audit/Reports). Built with React, TanStack Router, Vite, D1 (SQLite), and Cloudflare Workers.

nodes.label: Affinity Designer Source File
nodes.rationale: The file theobase.af in the branding directory is an Affinity Designer source file that likely contains the source art for the cover image and other branding assets.

nodes.label: Theobase Profile Picture (flame/torch icon in orange #F97316)
nodes.rationale: PNG image in branding directory alongside logo-light.svg, icon.svg, favicon.ico, cover.png, and theobase.af (Affinity Designer source). Companion icon.svg depicts a flame/torch icon in orange (#F97316) â€” the profile picture likely derives from the same icon asset. Serves as the application avatar/profile image for Theobase, a Seventh-day Adventist church administration platform spanning Conference â†’ District â†’ Church â†’ Member levels.
nodes.norm_label: theobase profile picture (flame/torch icon in orange #f97316)

nodes.label: Theobase Brand Palette: Orange #F97316 + Gray #6B7280
nodes.rationale: Brand colors extracted from companion SVG files. Logo-light.svg uses fill:rgb(249,115,22) for the flame/torch icon and fill:rgb(107,114,128) for the wordmark. The profile picture likely uses the same orange (#F97316) flame/torch icon.

nodes.label: Flame/Torch Icon â€” primary brand mark of Theobase
nodes.rationale: The companion icon.svg file depicts a flame/torch icon rendered in orange (#F97316). This icon serves as the primary brand mark across favicon.ico, logos, and the profile picture. The name 'Theobase' (theo = God) pairs with the flame as a symbol of the Holy Spirit / divine presence â€” thematically aligned with Seventh-day Adventist theology.
nodes.norm_label: flame/torch icon a€” primary brand mark of theobase

links.rationale: Cover image is a branding asset for the Theobase platform â€” directly extracted from file location in the branding directory.
Cover image likely uses the same brand colors (orange #F97316 + gray #6B7280) found in companion SVG files in the same directory.
The theobase.af Affinity Designer source file in the same directory likely contains the design source for cover.png.

All brand assets in the directory share a common Affinity Designer source file (theobase.af). The profile picture was likely exported from this source. INFERRED: presence of .af file in same directory is strong signal, but we cannot confirm the profile picture is an explicit artboard/export from it.

links.evidence: icon.svg is in branding/ directory of theobase repo; the path shape in icon.svg matches the icon portion of logo-light.svg which contains the full theobase wordmark
links.rationale: The icon is the brand mark of the Theobase project.

links.evidence: icon.svg path data (line 7) matches logo-light.svg path data (line 8) exactly at the coordinate level
links.rationale: The icon and logo-light share the identical shield/T-shaped path geometry.

links.evidence: SVG contains xmlns:serif='http://www.serif.com/' namespace; theobase.af (Serif Affinity format file) exists in branding/
links.rationale: The Serif namespace in SVG and companion .af file strongly indicate the SVG was exported from Affinity Designer.

links.evidence: style='fill:rgb(249,115,22)' on the path in icon.svg line 7
links.rationale: The dominant visual element is the orange fill color rgb(249,115,22), likely a primary brand color. This is a self-referencing property edge.

links.evidence: logo-light.svg is in branding/ directory; contains 'theobase' wordmark text in the second path group
links.rationale: The full logo represents the Theobase project.
Same Serif namespace indicates Affinity Designer as the export tool.

links.evidence: SVG depicts the Theobase logo mark and wordmark
SVG structure: first g transform group contains the T-shaped path geometry
SVG structure: second g transform group contains additional path data interpreting as text glyphs
SVG rect element with id logo at line 6 is a child of the root svg element
Stylized T-shape likely stands for the Theobase initial letter T
Path fill attribute is explicitly rgb(249,115,22)

links.rationale: Cannot view image directly. Profile picture in branding directory likely shares the flame/torch icon motif from icon.svg, especially if it's a cropped or scaled version of the same icon. INFERRED: 0.7 with -0.25 penalty for 'cannot verify visually', but strong circumstantial evidence from sibling files.
All brand assets use orange #F97316 and gray #6B7280. The profile picture PNG almost certainly uses these brand colors. High confidence given brand consistency patterns.
